    "R1.1": "The artifact leads with a strong contribution claim ('first system… to our knowledge') and a specific gap statement (calibrated abstention for general manipulation). Reconstructing whether the claim is well-scoped and the gap is real is central to any meaningful check.",
    "R1.2": "The abstention pipeline has a concrete logical chain: entropy H(p) → threshold tau → binary execute/abstain decision. Checking whether this chain is internally consistent (e.g., whether calibrating tau on a held-out split can actually bound false-execute rate without distributional assumptions) is a natural part of reviewing the paper.",
    "R1.3": "The paper makes four highly specific quantitative claims: 91% abstention on ambiguous, 88% correct on unambiguous, +27 pts vs. no-abstention baseline, +14 pts vs. confidence-threshold ablation. rationale.R1.1: The artifact leads with a strong contribution claim ('first system… to our knowledge') and a specific gap statement (calibrated abstention for general manipulation). Reconstructing whether the claim is well-scoped and the gap is real is central to any meaningful check.
rationale.R1.2: The abstention pipeline has a concrete logical chain: entropy H(p) → threshold tau → binary execute/abstain decision. Checking whether this chain is internally consistent (e.g., whether calibrating tau on a held-out split can actually bound false-execute rate without distributional assumptions) is a natural part of reviewing the paper.
rationale.R1.3: The paper makes four highly specific quantitative claims: 91% abstention on ambiguous, 88% correct on unambiguous, +27 pts vs. no-abstention baseline, +14 pts vs. confidence-threshold ablation. These numbers are load-bearing — if they are unsupported or cherry-picked, the paper's case collapses. Evidence-claim alignment is the highest-stakes dimension here.
rationale.R1.4: The 'first system, to our knowledge' assertion requires checking against nearest prior work (entropy-based abstention in classification, confidence-threshold robot policies, prior work on ambiguous instruction handling). Without that delta, novelty cannot be assessed.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on a specific dimension — e.g., whether the quantitative claims are internally consistent and well-supported (R1.3), whether the novelty claim holds against nearest prior work (R1.4), or whether the logical chain of the abstention rule is sound (R1.2) — or should I perform a broad cross-dimensional review covering all of these?
